title: Step 3: Follow the steps in the  window to get the connection string and run the connection string in your command line.

sections.heading: Configure Connection to Online Archive
sections.body:
You can set up a standard connection or private endpoint for connecting
to your online archives. To set up the connection method:

Configure your connection type.
To configure the type of connection:

- Choose one of the following options:

  - Standard connection

  - Private endpoint

- Set up your type of connection. To set up, for:

  <Tab tabid="standard">
    Standard Connection

    Click:

    - Add your current IP address to add your
      current IP address to the list of allowed IP addresses.

    - Add a different IP address to specify the
      IP address to add to the list of allowed IP addresses.

    - Allow access from anywhere to allow access
      from all IP addresses.
  </Tab>

  Private Endpoint

  Click Private endpoint and:

  - [Set Up a Private Endpoint for Online Archives](oa-config-private-endpoint)

  - [Set Up a Private Endpoint for Online Archives](oa-config-private-endpoint)

  - Use a private endpoint already [Set Up a Private Endpoint for Online Archives](oa-config-private-endpoint) for online archives

  > **Note:** If you specify `0.0.0.0` in the IP access list, Atlas
  > accepts all connections including those over PrivateLink.

To create a database user for your online archive:

- Specify Username.

- Enter Password or click Autogenerate secure
  password.

- Click Create database user.

sections.heading: Connect to Online Archive
sections.body:
You can use the connection string for an online archive to access and
query data in your online archive only.> **Note:** The connection string for an online archive
> is read-only. If you must write to your cluster,
> click **Connect to Cluster** and use the
> provided connection string instead.**Clusterspage:**Connect from Clusters PageTo get the connection string from the
**Clusters** page:You can connect through a Driver,
Compass, and the MongoDB Shell.**Oapage:**Connect from Online Archive PageTo get the connection string from the **Online Archive**
page:You can connect through a Driver,
Compass, and the MongoDB Shell.You can use this connection string to run read-only, federated
queries against your `Online Archive`.

sections.heading: Connect to Online Archive and Cluster
sections.body:
You can use the connection string for an online archive and cluster to
access and query data in your online archive and Atlas cluster.> **Note:** The connection string for an online archive and cluster
> is read-only. If you must write to your cluster,
> click **Connect to Cluster** and use the
> provided connection string instead.**Clusterspage:**Connect from Clusters PageTo get the connection string from the **Clusters** page:You can connect through a Driver,
Compass, and the MongoDB Shell.**Oapage:**Connect from Online Archive PageTo get the connection string from the **Online Archive**
page:You can connect through a Driver,
Compass, and the MongoDB Shell.

sections.heading: Connect to the Federated Database Instance for Your Online Archive
sections.body:
You can use the federated database instance connection string for your online archive to
access and query data in your online archive only.

To get the connection string from the `Data Federation` page:

You can connect through a Driver, Compass, and the MongoDB Shell.

You can also connect to the Online Archive using [OIDC](oidc-authentication-authorization).
